claim: No Stable, release, conformance, package-parity, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: React Aria NumberField with an input spinbutton and canonical hidden form control
semantics.naming: A visible Field label, native label association, aria-label, or aria-labelledby names the spinbutton.
semantics.roleOverride: Consumer role overrides are outside the supported contract.
semantics.refTarget: HTMLDivElement root; inputRef reaches the editable HTMLInputElement

interaction.keyboard: Locale-aware editing, composition, increment, decrement, Home, End, Page Up, and Page Down follow React Aria semantics; the optional scrub button mirrors step commands.
interaction.pointer: Stepper buttons perform discrete changes; the optional scrub button uses primary horizontal pointer capture with cancellation.
interaction.touch: Coarse-pointer controls preserve at least a 44 CSS pixel preferred target.
interaction.speech: Visible labels and localized increment, decrement, and scrub names remain available for voice targeting.
interaction.switchAccess: Every value-changing affordance is a real focusable input or button.

formBehavior: When name is supplied, React Aria owns one hidden successful control whose value is a canonical ASCII number; reset restores the uncontrolled default. Disabled fields are omitted.

states.synchronization: Visible localized text, numeric state, step controls, validation, and hidden form serialization share one React Aria state engine.

preferences.enhancedContrast: Uses semantic tokens and non-color state cues.
preferences.forcedColors: Uses system colors, visible borders, and system-color focus indicators.
preferences.reducedMotion: No essential information or interaction depends on animation.

responsive.overflowPolicy: Controls retain operability and values scroll within the input without clipping surrounding labels or messages.

internationalization.imePolicy: The editable input retains React Aria composition handling and does not commit partial IME text.
internationalization.serialization: Visible parsing and formatting use the provider locale; controlled values and hidden form serialization remain canonical JavaScript numbers.

consumerResponsibilities: Provide a persistent visible label and describe units when they are not otherwise clear.
Choose minimum, maximum, step, and precision values that match the domain rather than using visual formatting as validation.
Do not enable wheel changes where page scrolling could cause accidental edits.
Do not depend on localized visible text as a machine-readable submitted value.
limitations: Source-present-unreleased records do not include immutable generated artifacts, packed-consumer evidence, package/source parity, or current manual assistive-technology sessions.
